formance Of their respective duties

e. 8. Potts has been elected by the
trades council to represent the Build
ing Laborers' International Protect
i've union, Ne. 2.


T. 9, Heskett, J. T. Snyder, w. III.
Metcalf and w. H. Upper are the
new delegates to the council from
the Typographical union, No. 1913.


The new officers of State Employees
union. No. %, were installed last
evening. The meeting was unusually
well attended and at its conclusion
the boys prepared an impromptu
entertainment. rendering a program
that consisted of all the latest songs
and jokes.
